findings.message: User-supplied input passed to untransformed create_item() instead of create_item_normalized(). Repository explicitly requires normalized input: 'Callers that accept user input MUST go through this function, not the bare create_item().' This breaks the data lineage contract and stores unvalidated user data.
findings.suggestion: Replace create_item(session, payload.name) with create_item_normalized(session, payload.name) on line 45 to ensure consistent data transformation and comply with the documented contract.

findings.message: Docstring explicitly acknowledges the data lineage violation: 'the normalisation step (strip + lowercase) is skipped so names are stored with whatever casing and whitespace the caller sends.' This documents a data consistency issue rather than fixing it.
findings.suggestion: Remove the acknowledgment of the skipped transformation and instead implement the required normalization step. Update docstring to describe the actual normalized behavior after fix.
